svg.text: 09 - Roadmap and Implementation Order
What this covers: phased delivery plan, done criteria, and execution risks for a two-person team.
Roadmap Philosophy
Build minimum proof of core value first: AI planning + adaptive live companion. Delay non-core polish until value is real.
Phase 1 (Weeks 1-3): Foundation
Build substrate:
project scaffolding, CI/CD baseline, DB schema, auth, basic FE shell, deployment path.
Definition of done:
user can authenticate, reach dashboard, and use starter chat surface against live backend.
Phase 2 (Weeks 4-8): Planning AI
Build planning loop:
structured conversation, attraction curation, route building, itinerary generation, editable review UX.
Definition of done:
complete NZ planning conversation yields persisted day-by-day itinerary that users can inspect/edit.
Phase 3 (Weeks 9-14): Live Companion
Build adaptation engine:
condition integrations, scoring, briefings, notifications, swap evaluation + acceptance flow.
Definition of done:
active trip receives reliable daily briefings and meaningful swap proposals based on real conditions.
Phase 4 (Weeks 15-18): Polish + Launch
stability tuning, UX polish, onboarding quality, performance hardening, soft launch execution.
Definition of done: cohesive end-to-end user journey is demo-worthy and robust for real testers.
Implementation Dependency Order
Priority path for maximal learning speed:
1) Auth + trip persistence + itinerary schema
2) Planning conversation orchestration with structured selections
3) Route/itinerary generation engine + interactive dashboard
4) Condition ingestion + scoring + forecast confidence model
5) Morning briefing generator + push delivery
6) Swap engine + acceptance/decline state transitions
7) Fine-grained guidance, hidden gems, storytelling refinements
Risk Mitigation Plan
AI quality risk -> start strongest model on critical tasks; optimize later.
API cost risk -> use free/fallback providers + aggressive caching from day one.
scope creep risk -> phase gates with strict done definitions.
team capacity risk -> prioritize features that validate core hypothesis first.
What Not To Build Early
- broad multi-destination expansion before NZ quality is proven
- deep marketplace/affiliate complexity before companion loop works
- advanced social features before planning/adaptation retention is validated
Success Metrics and Gates
MVP gate (end of Phase 4)
test users complete full planning + companion workflow with clear perceived uplift vs baseline tools.
at least one verified high-value swap event captured with positive user outcome.
Growth gate (Phase 5+)
increasing monthly itinerary count, strong swap acceptance, briefing satisfaction, readiness for second destination.
Execution Cadence
weekly goals mapped to phase scope, with explicit "defer" decisions for non-core asks.
end-of-week demos must show user-visible progress, not only backend plumbing.
Failure signals to watch early
users abandon planning flow mid-way, low trust in swaps, generic briefings, poor editability of itinerary.
Phase 5 direction
new destinations, mobile app track, revenue features (premium/B2B/affiliate), advanced intelligence upgrades.
Practical Build Advice for This Repo
Treat each phase boundary as a production-quality API and data contract checkpoint.
When time is tight, prioritize end-to-end thin slices over isolated subsystem perfection.
Track explicit "why deferred" decisions to avoid stealth scope growth.
Next recommended read: 10 - UX Information Architecture.
